fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Front Driveway; Vehicle Parking Area
fr.considerations: Single Access Point; Limited Side Access; Dense Vegetation
fr.recommendations: Improving emergency response effectiveness at this property could be supported by enhancing the accessibility and visibility of key areas. Establishing a clear and wide path from the single access point to the main building may facilitate smoother entry and movement for responders. Managing dense vegetation along the driveway and around the structure would assist in maintaining unobstructed lines of sight, increasing visibility, and reducing potential obstructions. Additionally, clearly delineating the vehicle parking area to prevent blockage of the driveway and main access routes could support staging and maneuverability for emergency vehicles. Enhancing side access, where feasible, might also provide responders with alternative routes to approach and navigate the property efficiently.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Defensible Space; Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space could be achieved by increasing separation between tree clusters and structures, while managing the scattered trees to minimize fuel continuity. Pruning or relocating vegetation adjacent to structures would reduce potential exposure to flames and embers. Preserving existing defensible space and actively maintaining it ensures greater separation from potential fire sources. Addressing the vegetated perimeter by thinning or selectively removing vegetation can limit fire spread and provide a buffer. Emphasizing vegetation management along the access route not only enhances safety for evacuation but also improves firefighter accessibility and movement. By focusing on reducing vegetation concentrations around the property and maintaining open spaces, the resilience of the property against wildfires may be improved.
